text.title: Code Review Checklist
text.summary: Six review dimensions — correctness, design, maintainability, testing, performance, security — each with concrete, testable checks.

list.item: Does the code do what the ticket/spec says?
Are all edge cases handled (empty input, null, max values)?
Is error handling present and appropriate for each failure mode?
Are there obvious bugs visible from reading the diff?
Is the code well-organized and consistent with project patterns?
Is there unnecessary complexity or over-abstraction?
Are responsibilities properly separated (no leaky abstractions)?
Does it follow the project's module/layer conventions?
Are names meaningful and self-documenting?
Would a new team member understand the code in isolation?
Is complex logic documented with inline comments explaining why, not what?
Are there sufficient tests for the changed code?
Do tests cover edge cases and error paths?
Are tests readable — do they document intent?
Do tests actually verify behavior, not just execute code?
Any obvious O(n^2) or worse algorithms on unbounded input?
N+1 query patterns in loops over ORM results?
Missing database indexes for new query patterns?
Unnecessary memory allocations or copies in hot paths?
Input validation on all external inputs?
No hardcoded secrets or credentials?
SQL queries parameterized (no string concatenation)?
Proper authentication and authorization checks?
XSS prevention for user-generated content rendered in HTML?

rule.statement: Cap PR size at 400 changed lines before the agent reviews; above that, ask the author to split the PR first.
rule.rationale: SmartBear empirical research shows review quality degrades significantly past 400 LoC and 60 minutes; agents silently truncate large diffs.
